task_summary: SP20a human watch terminal surface: --format human with --watch or --watch-count and no --output now prints the terminal-first human surface per iteration instead of JSONL watch records.

verification_evidence.summary: 31 passed after applying source, focused tests, and README usage edits.

implemented_contract.human_watch_stdout: When --format human is used with --watch or --watch-count and no --output, each foreground iteration writes render_terminal_human output directly to stdout.
implemented_contract.state_watch_preserved: --format state watch output remains newline-delimited JSONL watch records.
implemented_contract.html_watch_preserved: --format html --output PATH continues rewriting the same local HTML file and emitting watch records.
implemented_contract.bounded_watch: --watch-count N still performs exactly N foreground iterations and exits.
implemented_contract.unbounded_watch: --watch without --watch-count still runs in the foreground until KeyboardInterrupt and returns cleanly.
implemented_contract.launcher_live_safety: The slice does not change launcher execution gates, live-active-window opt-in, live-desk opt-in, or hidden/background/network safety fields.

covered_cases: human --watch-count prints the terminal human header twice for two iterations
human --watch-count includes foreground-watch freshness metadata for iteration 0 and 1
human --watch-count stdout omits JSON record_type and schema_version markers
human --watch runs until injected KeyboardInterrupt and prints two terminal surfaces
state JSONL watch records still parse as JSON and expose watch_iteration values
html output watch still rewrites the target file with latest iteration freshness
README includes the --format human --watch-count usage command

acceptance_notes: The implementation intentionally keeps the prior HTML renderer and default html format intact.
The change is scoped under experiments/mew-ghost only.
The report is local dogfood evidence for the SP20a practical slice.
remaining_risk: No broad HTML removal was attempted; this slice only changes human watch stdout behavior when no output file is requested.
